name: Madison by Lyzr
description: The Agentic Banking OS by Lyzr: governed AI agents across banking functions — commercial banking, compliant marketing, deal intelligence, mortgage-servicing dispute resolution, HR, and risk & compliance. Deployed inside the institution's perimeter, human-in-the-loop on every regulated step, with a full exportable audit trail. Enterprise, quote-based; access is provisioned per engagement, not via a public endpoint.

onboarding.howToStart: Madison is deployed inside a bank's perimeter and provisioned per engagement, beginning with a design-partner pilot. There is no public API, OAuth flow, or MCP endpoint. To start, book a demo.

skills.name: Commercial Banking (RM)
skills.description: Prepare relationship managers for client meetings and coordinate credit, service, and portfolio work on the systems the bank already runs.
examples: Prepare a decision-ready brief for a commercial client meeting.

skills.name: Compliant Marketing
skills.description: Turn a one-line brief into a compliant, launch-ready, multi-channel campaign on the bank's own data, with fair-lending guardrails and independent human approval.
examples: Launch a compliant high-yield savings campaign to eligible members.

skills.name: Deal Intelligence
skills.description: Evaluate companies, partners, and targets with evidence, context, and permissioned deal memory; resolve to partner / invest / acquire / monitor / pass.
examples: Evaluate a payments fintech as a potential partner.

skills.name: Dispute Resolution (Mortgage Servicing)
skills.description: Investigate and draft mortgage-servicing disputes inside the RESPA window; a deterministic rulebook decides, the model drafts, a person signs off.
examples: Resolve an escrow-shortage dispute with a compliant borrower response.

skills.name: HR
skills.description: Run the employee journey: candidate intake and scoring, an early phone screen, an employee desk that acts in the HRMS, and offboarding.
examples: Score a requisition's applicants and produce a shortlist a recruiter can trust.

skills.name: Risk & Compliance
skills.description: Agentic third-party risk, vendor-AI and model-risk governance, and generated, stress-tested vendor exit playbooks — on-prem and examiner-ready.
examples: Govern vendor-embedded AI with an enforceable kill-switch and audit trail.
